name: Ubiquiti airMAX NanoStation M5
title: Ubiquiti airMAX NanoStation M5
title_extended: Ubiquiti NanoStation M5 5 GHz Outdoor Wireless Bridge
short_description: 5170-5875 MHz, 14.6-16.1 dBi antenna, AirOS, POE-RJ-45, 802.11a/n, 2x GE ports
description: The Ubiquiti airMAX NanoStation M5 (NSM5) exemplifies the cutting-edge in compact, low cost outdoor wireless communication devices, engineered to excel in a wide variety of applications including point-to-point (PtP) or point-to-multipoint (PtMP) networks with a up to 15km+ range depending on the setup. Its core technology, the integrated airMAX® protocol based on MIMO TDMA, enhances network performance and scalability by efficiently managing data transmission and reducing latency.

detailed_description.heading: Technology and Applications
detailed_description.text: The Ubiquiti airMAX NanoStation M5 (NSM5) exemplifies the cutting-edge in compact, low cost outdoor wireless communication devices, engineered to excel in a wide variety of applications including point-to-point (PtP) or point-to-multipoint (PtMP) networks with a up to 15km+ range depending on the setup. Its core technology, the integrated airMAX® protocol based on MIMO TDMA, enhances network performance and scalability by efficiently managing data transmission and reducing latency. This technology is a linchpin for establishing high-capacity networks that serve multiple clients with minimized interference, a crucial aspect for dense deployment scenarios.

detailed_description.heading: Standalone and Integrated Operation
detailed_description.text: The Ubiquiti airMAX NanoStation M5 can operate both as a standalone device or in conjunction with other devices, depending on the specific application or networking requirement. As a standalone unit, the NSM5 can function effectively in a point-to-point (PtP) setup. This scenario is common for connecting two separate locations, such as two buildings within a campus or across a distance in a rural area, where direct cable connection is impractical or impossible. In this configuration, two NSM5 units would be aimed at each other to create a wireless bridge, enabling high-speed data transmission over the air. However, the NSM5 truly excels when connected to other devices as part of a broader network infrastructure. In a point-to-multipoint (PtMP) setup, one NSM5 unit can serve as the central access point (or base station), connecting to multiple NSM5 units configured as clients. This arrangement is ideal for providing internet or network access to multiple buildings or locations from a single source, making it highly versatile for applications ranging from internet service provision to campus-wide network distribution.

detailed_description.heading: 5GHz Performance
detailed_description.text: Operating on the 5GHz frequency, the NSM5 is adept at avoiding the congested 2.4GHz band, paving the way for clearer transmission channels and less interference in urban environments. This frequency band, coupled with the device's 16dBi high-gain antenna, permits long-distance wireless connections, which are further bolstered by the device's capability to achieve 150+ Mbps real outdoor throughput. Such performance metrics signify the NanoStation M5's ability to support data-intensive applications, including HD video streaming and VoIP services, over considerable distances. Also, the NanoStation M5 can be used as an access point, connecting to hundreds of devices all at the same time, or as a client, which can be connected to other networks through a bridge. The level of flexibility makes this a fantastic and convenient addition to your networking system.

detailed_description.heading: Design and Connectivity
detailed_description.text: The physical design of the NSM5 reflects Ubiquiti's commitment to durability and functionality. Measuring 294 x 31 x 80 mm and weighing 400 g, its compact and lightweight form factor does not compromise its robustness, evidenced by its outdoor UV stabilized plastic enclosure. This ensures the device's longevity and reliability when deployed in varied environmental conditions, with operational temperatures ranging from -30 to 75°C and a humidity tolerance of 5 to 95% noncondensing. Connectivity and power supply considerations are seamlessly addressed through the inclusion of dual 10/100 Ethernet ports and support for passive PoE, facilitating ease of installation and the flexibility to power secondary devices directly through the NSM5.
